sections.paragraph:
Ζαβουλών, ὁ indecl. (זְבוּלוּן) (LXX; JosAs 27:6; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 5 Jac.; Philo, Fuga 73; Test12Patr. Joseph. acc. to his ordinary usage would prob. decline Ζαβουλών [Ant. 1, 308; 2, 179]-ῶνος) Zebulun (Gen 30:20), an Israelite tribe Rv 7:8; its territory (beside Naphtali) Mt 4:13, 15; Lk 4:31 D.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

acc. to acc. to = according to

prob. prob. = probable, probably

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 424
